situation: COGALPHA-03 (audit-2026-07-07-028): Agent::build_prompt interpolates user/external-controlled context fields (universe, factor_stats, seed_expressions) directly into LLM prompt with no sanitization. Attacker controlling context JSON can inject system-level instructions to manipulate factor generation.
action: Add sanitize_context_field() helper that strips ASCII control characters and caps length per field. Restructure build_prompt output with explicit [SYSTEM INSTRUCTIONS — TRUSTED] / [USER DATA — UNTRUSTED CONTEXT] boundary markers so the model can distinguish trusted instructions from untrusted data. Cap universe at 200 chars, factor_stats at 2000, each seed_expression at 200 (max 10 seeds). Added 2 new tests validating control-char stripping and length capping.
outcome_evidence: cargo test -p moni-cogalpha -- agents::tests: 6/6 passed (including 2 new tests build_prompt_strips_control_chars_and_caps_length + build_prompt_includes_seed_expressions with boundary marker assertions). Committed e5339c0 on auto/opt-cogalpha-1783357989, pushed to origin.
next_suggestion: Dispatch next pending derived_fix: COGALPHA-04 (silent fallback to MockLlmClient masks production misconfiguration, llm.rs:163, medium direction-shift)

audit_findings.finding: Prompt injection via user-controlled context fields in Agent::build_prompt — unsanitized interpolation of universe/factor_stats/seed_expressions allows injected system-level instructions
audit_findings.remediation: sanitize_context_field() helper strips control chars + caps length; structured prompt with [SYSTEM INSTRUCTIONS — TRUSTED] / [USER DATA — UNTRUSTED CONTEXT] boundary markers
